name: Volusion E-Commerce Platform API
what_it_does: Hosted e-commerce platform with REST API — enabling small to mid-size merchants to manage product catalogs, orders, customers, and store operations programmatically, with V2 API offering GraphQL for storefront data and REST for admin operations, targeted at businesses migrating away from Volusion or needing custom integrations.
use_cases: Syncing product catalog data via Volusion API for multi-channel inventory management
Retrieving order data via Volusion API for fulfillment automation integrations
Managing customer data via Volusion API for CRM integration
Building custom storefront via Volusion V2 API for headless commerce implementation
Migrating store data via Volusion API for platform transition projects
not_for: New enterprise commerce deployments (Shopify Plus, BigCommerce for growth; Magento for enterprise)
Developers wanting developer-first API infrastructure (Volusion is SMB-focused SaaS)
Complex B2B commerce with custom pricing, quotes, and ERP integration
best_when: An existing Volusion merchant needs to integrate their store with third-party tools, automate operations, or is building a custom storefront layer on top of their Volusion store data.
avoid_when: Starting a new e-commerce store (better options exist: Shopify, WooCommerce); need enterprise B2B features; or are migrating away from Volusion and don't need the API after.

auth.notes: OAuth2 for V2 API (access token via client credentials or authorization code). V1 legacy API uses API key. V2 uses Bearer token. Webhooks for order/product/customer events. No fine-grained OAuth scopes. Partner app registration required for OAuth. V1 and V2 APIs coexist — different auth mechanisms. Webhook HMAC signature validation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier for API; requires Volusion store subscription
pricing.notes: Austin, Texas. Founded 1999. Filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy 2020, emerged same year. Now Volusion LLC (private). One of oldest US e-commerce platforms (older than Shopify). 30,000+ active merchants (down from 180K+ peak). SMB-focused. Legacy platform — many merchants migrating away. V2 API launched 2020 post-bankruptcy (more modern). Payment processing via Volusion Payments (Stripe-backed). Historically had security incidents (2019 Magecart attack). Limited third-party app ecosystem compared to Shopify.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: V1 (API key) and V2 (OAuth2) APIs coexist. V2 adds GraphQL for storefront. Webhooks with HMAC. PCI-DSS. US data residency. Historically declining platform — verify merchant is still on Volusion before building integrations.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Product/order reads idempotent by ID. Order creation not natively idempotent — check before creating. Customer reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Two API versions (V1 and V2) with different auth — V1 uses API key, V2 uses OAuth2
Platform filed Chapter 11 in 2020 — verify merchant viability before deep integration
Declining merchant base — many merchants actively migrating to Shopify/WooCommerce
2019 Magecart security incident — platform has had security issues historically
V2 GraphQL is storefront-only (public data) — admin operations use V2 REST or V1 REST
Limited app ecosystem — fewer third-party integrations compared to Shopify

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. PCI-DSS. OAuth2 for V2. Historical security incident (2019 Magecart JS injection). Post-bankruptcy security improvements made. US data residency. Webhook HMAC validation. Platform viability consideration — bankruptcy history increases vendor risk.
